cards.title: Pet Ownership Teaches Family Nurturing Skills
cards.body: Raising a pet rabbit taught parents as much about nurturing as caring for their babies. The experience highlighted how to care for creatures you can't reason with, providing unexpected parenting lessons.

cards.title: Parenting Teenagers Through A-Levels
cards.body: Supporting a teenager through A-levels can leave parents feeling powerless and overwhelmed. Helping with subjects like chemistry and further maths reveals the limits of parental involvement in academic pressure.

cards.title: Reading Together Helps Parents Grieve
cards.body: Reading with his son from his late wife's book collection helped a widowed father process grief and guilt. Sharing this world of words with his child became a healing practice.

cards.title: Family Estrangement Effects on Child Wellbeing
cards.body: Family estrangement exists on a spectrum, not as a binary choice, and affects wellbeing in mixed ways. Support systems play a crucial role whether families seek reconciliation or not.

cards.title: Stop Limiting Screen Time, Build Thinking Skills
cards.body: Parents often focus on restricting screen time when they should prioritize building children's capacity to think. The answer lies in older, proven methods that develop genuine cognitive ability.

cards.title: When Children Don't Feel They Matter
cards.body: For children to develop healthy self-esteem, parents must actively monitor how their kids feel about themselves. Parental awareness of a child's emotional state is fundamental to their wellbeing.

cards.title: Humility in Parenting Adolescents Reduces Pressure
cards.body: Parents who maintain modest expectations about their influence on adolescent development reduce pressure on themselves. This realistic approach strengthens the parent-child relationship during turbulent years.

cards.title: Middle Childhood Reshapes Families Profoundly
cards.body: Middle childhood is an overlooked period that fundamentally reshapes children, parents, and family connections. This developmental stage deserves greater attention and understanding from parents.

cards.title: Post-Mortem Delays Leave Families Grieving
cards.body: A shortage of pathologists forces families to wait months for post-mortem examination results after losing a child. The emotional toll on grieving families is severe and unacceptable.
